description: Expand DesignConstants with a comprehensive design system and polish the core chat and agent views for a refined, consistent macOS experience. This is the foundation that all other views depend on.
steps: STEP 1 - Expand DesignConstants.swift (Sources/KiroKantoku/Views/Components/DesignConstants.swift): Add new design tokens for a cohesive system. Add: (a) Button-specific constants: buttonCornerRadius = 6, buttonPaddingH = 12, buttonPaddingV = 6, buttonMinHeight = 28 (comfortable but not oversized). (b) Card constants: cardCornerRadius = 10 (unified - no mixed 10/12), cardPadding = 14, cardShadowRadius = 3, cardShadowY = 1.5, cardBorderOpacity = 0.12. (c) Input constants: inputCornerRadius = 8, inputMinHeight = 36, inputPaddingH = 10, inputPaddingV = 8. (d) Popup/sheet constants: sheetCornerRadius = 12, popoverShadowRadius = 12, popoverShadowOpacity = 0.15. (e) Badge constants: badgeCornerRadius = 5, badgePaddingH = 6, badgePaddingV = 2, badgeFontSize = 10. (f) Typography: captionSecondaryOpacity = 0.7, headingWeight = Font.Weight.semibold. (g) Animation: standardDuration = 0.2, hoverScale = 1.015 (subtle, not 1.02). (h) Semantic colors as computed properties: subtleBackground (windowBackgroundColor), cardBackground (controlBackgroundColor), inputBackground (controlBackgroundColor with slight opacity adjustment), accentSubtle (accentColor at 0.08 opacity), dangerSubtle (red at 0.08), warningSubtle (orange at 0.08), successSubtle (green at 0.08). (i) Layout: sidebarMinWidth = 220, detailMinWidth = 400, chatMinWidth = 320.

STEP 2 - Polish ChatInputView.swift: (a) Replace the raw TextEditor with a properly styled input area: add a subtle 1px border using separatorColor at 0.3 opacity around the text editor, use inputCornerRadius from DesignConstants. (b) Give the text editor a minimum height of DesignConstants.inputMinHeight and improve the inner padding to inputPaddingH/inputPaddingV. (c) Style the send button: use accentColor instead of raw .blue, ensure it has proper hit target (at least 36x36). (d) Style the photo picker button consistently with the send button sizing. (e) Add a subtle background to the entire input bar area using cardBackground. (f) Ensure the image attachment thumbnails use consistent cornerRadius from DesignConstants (cornerRadiusMedium not hardcoded 6). (g) Fix the slash picker and options picker overlays to use popoverShadowRadius/popoverShadowOpacity from DesignConstants instead of hardcoded shadow values.

STEP 3 - Polish ChatMessageView.swift: (a) Change user message background from raw .blue to .accentColor for system-appropriate theming. (b) Adjust the bubble cornerRadius: use a slightly asymmetric approach - 16pt on three corners, 4pt on the corner closest to the sender (bottom-right for user, bottom-left for assistant) using UnevenRoundedRectangle if available on macOS 14+, or keep uniform 16 if simpler. (c) Add a very subtle shadow to message bubbles (radius 2, opacity 0.06). (d) Improve assistant message background: use DesignConstants.cardBackground instead of raw controlBackgroundColor, add a very subtle border (separatorColor at 0.15 opacity, lineWidth 0.5). (e) Ensure consistent spacing between messages using DesignConstants.spacingMD (12) matching what ChatPanel already uses in its LazyVStack.

STEP 4 - Polish ChatPanel.swift: (a) The TypingIndicator: change the bubble background to use DesignConstants.cardBackground, use cornerRadiusMedium (not hardcoded 16) for a more refined look. (b) ContextUsageBar: increase the bar height from 4 to 5, use cornerRadiusSmall for the bar rounding, add a subtle transition animation when the percentage changes. (c) StatusBannerView: use DesignConstants.cardCornerRadius for the roundedness, ensure the background opacity is consistent (0.06 instead of 0.05). (d) OAuthRequestView: use DesignConstants constants for corner radius and padding instead of hardcoded values. (e) Clean up the connecting state: ensure the spinner + 'Connecting...' text is centered with proper spacing.

STEP 5 - Polish InlineToolCallView.swift: (a) Use DesignConstants.cardBackground instead of controlBackgroundColor.opacity(0.6). (b) Ensure the expanded content area uses consistent padding from DesignConstants. (c) Use badgeCornerRadius for the location badges instead of hardcoded 3. (d) Add a subtle divider between the header and the expanded content for visual separation.

STEP 6 - Polish ThoughtBubbleView.swift and PlanView.swift: (a) ThoughtBubbleView: change background from purple.opacity(0.04) to a slightly warmer purple.opacity(0.05), add a subtle left border accent (2pt purple bar) for visual distinction, ensure the expanded content has a maxHeight with scrollability. (b) PlanView: use DesignConstants.cardBackground for the background, add subtle inner padding to the plan entries list, ensure the progress count badge uses DesignConstants.badgeCornerRadius and badgePadding.

STEP 7 - Polish PermissionRequestView.swift: (a) Use DesignConstants.cardCornerRadius (10) instead of hardcoded 10 for the card - keep the value but use the constant for consistency. (b) Ensure button padding uses DesignConstants.buttonPaddingH/V. (c) Use .controlSize(.small) consistently on all the option buttons. (d) Add .buttonStyle(.borderedProminent) for the allow buttons to make them more prominent, keeping .bordered for reject buttons. The tint already handles green/red coloring.

STEP 8 - Polish ConfigSelectorBar.swift and SkillsPanel.swift: (a) ConfigSelectorBar: add a subtle top border (Divider or separatorColor line) to visually separate it from the chat content above. (b) SkillsPanel: use DesignConstants.badgeCornerRadius for the 'Active' badge, ensure the 'Use' button uses .buttonStyle(.bordered) with .controlSize(.small) consistently, add subtle background to the disclosure group when expanded.
acceptance_criteria: DesignConstants.swift contains all new design tokens listed in step 1
ChatInputView uses DesignConstants for all spacing, corner radii, and shadow values - no hardcoded magic numbers for visual properties
ChatMessageView uses .accentColor for user bubbles instead of .blue
All inline tool calls, thought bubbles, plan views use DesignConstants for backgrounds and corner radii
PermissionRequestView buttons use DesignConstants for padding values
No visual inconsistency between curved and straight edges - all containers use consistent corner radii from DesignConstants
verification: Run 'swift build' and confirm it succeeds with exit code 0
Run 'swift test' and confirm all existing tests still pass
